body_txt: A snappy, one-liner comment just before the commercial break or opening sequence. Often takes this form:  The cut is what differentiates this from a standard One-Liner . Commonly found on detective/mystery series; a frequent (but not exclusive) use of this is when the detectives make a pithy Gallows Humor comment about the crime of the week in the Cold Open , followed by a cut to the credits. The usual purpose is to convey a sense that the detective has seen this level of horror before , and that he's so used to it he can make a joke about it. Often will include a Glasses Pull for emphasis. Due to the CSI-verse 's abuse of this trope, it's also become a Stock Parody of the franchise. Used frequently enough, the audience can be trained to expect one of these. Comments after the one-liner are usually a gag. Failure to make the one-liner means the situation is serious. Real Life use by actual police detectives is seriously frowned on, as it is considered disrespectful to the deceased and/or their families.
